extracted_claims: Real-rate moves can affect valuation-sensitive sectors more than the broad market.
evidence_items.methodology_summary: Empirical macro-finance framing using historical yield and asset-return relationships.
evidence_items.relevance_to_hypothesis: Directly relevant to Investigate semis versus defensives..
evidence_items.short_claim_summary: Falling real yields tend to support duration-sensitive equity segments, though the effect is regime-dependent.
evidence_items.title: Real-rate sensitivity of long-duration assets

extracted_claims: Macro interpretation matters as much as the raw data direction.
evidence_items.methodology_summary: Cross-market commentary supported by historical sector-relative performance comparisons.
evidence_items.relevance_to_hypothesis: Useful counterweight because it highlights conditional interpretation rather than a one-way macro rule.
evidence_items.short_claim_summary: Cooling inflation alone is often insufficient; growth-sensitive outperformance depends on whether the market reads the move as benign easing rather than growth deterioration.
evidence_items.source: Sell-side or public market strategy research
evidence_items.title: Sector rotation depends on growth expectations as well as inflation cooling

extracted_claims: Announcement effects are strongest when the surprise component is large.
evidence_items.methodology_summary: Event-study style measurement around announcement dates.
evidence_items.relevance_to_hypothesis: Relevant if the hypothesis is framed around CPI prints, labor releases, or other event windows.
evidence_items.short_claim_summary: Event-style reactions to macro releases exist, but persistence and sign can vary with the prevailing regime and expectations backdrop.
evidence_items.title: Macro announcement effects are short horizon and noisy across regimes
